get.description: Get the list of all categories.

post.description: Create a category.

get.description: Get root category.
Get category tree.
Get a category.
Get permissions of a category.

post.summary: Set the DOI of an artifact
post.description: Set the DOI annotation of an artifact. The user must have 'write' access to the artifact. The resource type must support DOIs, and an existing DOI can not be altered.

post.summary: Attach multiple categories to an artifact
post.description: Attach a list of existing categories to an existing artifact. The user must have 'write' access to the artifact. The user must have 'attach' access to all the categories. The call will exit at the firts category without 'attach' access
Attach an existing category to an existing artifact. The user must have 'write' access to the artifact. The user must have 'attach' access to the category.
Endpoint called by the Keycloak Event Listener. Creates the CEDAR objects related to a user (home folder, group membership) upon authentication.

post.summary: Check whether a template can be updated
post.description: Check whether an existing template can be updated with the supplied template definition. The destructive and non-destructive changes between the stored template and the supplied template are computed, and the number of affected instances is reported.
Convert the resource supplied in the request body to the requested output format.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Output format type to display the content of the template instance. The allowed values are: 'jsonld', 'json', 'rdf-nquad'

post.description: Copy artifact to a given folder. A copy of the given artifact will be created in the given folder, with a new name Only artifacts (fields, elements, templates, instances) can be copied.

Create draft artifact out of a published artifact. A new artifact will be created in the supplied folder. The version of the new artifact must be set, and must follow the current published version. The sharing settings of the old artifact can be copied over to the new artifact..
Detach an existing category from an existing artifact. The user must have 'write' access to the artifact. The user must have 'attach' access to the category.

Regenerate rules index. This is an administrative command, you need special permission to run it. The old index will be deleted and replaced by a new empty one. The recommendations will work, but will not return results, since the index will be empty.

Empty search index. This is an administrative command, you need special permission to run it. The old index will be deleted and replaced by a new empty one. The search will work, but will not return results, since the index will be empty.

post.summary: Preview the inclusion subgraph of an artifact
post.description: Build a preview of the tree of artifacts affected by a change to the given artifact, without applying any update.

post.summary: Update the inclusion subgraph of an artifact
post.description: Propagate a change to the given artifact across the tree of affected artifacts, updating each referencing artifact on the artifact server.

post.summary: Load the OWL value sets ontology generated by the caDSR CDE injestor.
post.description: Load the OWL value sets ontology generated by the NCI caDSR CDE injestor. Each time the ingestor runs (typically nightly) and new value sets ontology is created, which descibed the value sets used by caDSR CDEs.

get.summary: Get the status of the value sets ontology loading
get.description: Get the current status of the value sets ontology loading process.

post.description: Make artifact not open.

parameters.name: idRequest
parameters.description: Id of the artifact to make open

post.description: Make artifact open.

parameters.name: idRequest
parameters.description: Id of the artifact to make open

post.description: Make folder not open.

parameters.name: idRequest
parameters.description: Id of the folder to make open

post.description: Make folder open.

parameters.name: idRequest
parameters.description: Id of the folder to make open

post.description: Move resource to a given folder. Folders or artifacts (fields, elements, templates, instances) can be moved.
Publish artifact. The 'bibo:status' of the artifact will be changed from 'bibo:draft' to 'bibo:published'. The 'pav:version' will be also set.

post.summary: Publish a template and create a new draft
post.description: Publish the given template, then create a new draft version from it and apply the supplied template definition. Instances of the source template can be copied into a new folder.

parameters.name: folder_name
parameters.description: Name of the folder to copy the template instances into.

post.description: Regenerate rules index. This is an administrative command, you need special permission to run it. If there is no need for reindexing, the task will not run. It can be forced using the force parameter. A background task will be started. The status of the task can be monitored in the resource server log files. While the reindexing runs, the old index is still used for searching. Once the new index is ready, the old one will be removed, and the new one will be used for searching. The reindexing computes permissions, it can run for a long time.

Regenerate search index. This is an administrative command, you need special permission to run it. If there is no need for reindexing, the task will not run. It can be forced using the force parameter. A background task will be started. The status of the task can be monitored in the resource server log files. While the reindexing runs, the old index is still used for searching. Once the new index is ready, the old one will be removed, and the new one will be used for searching. The reindexing computes permissions, it can run for a long time.
Change name and/or description of a resource. Folders or artifacts (fields, elements, templates, instances) can be altered.

Validate CEDAR resources (i.e., templates, elements and instances) against the CEDAR meta-model. To use this service you will need to append the resource text in the request body as the payload. However, in the case of validating the template instance, you have an additional option to include the template text by organizing them as follows: { "schema": <template text>, "instance": <instance text> }. The validation service will return a report in JSON format as follows: { "validates": "", "warnings": [], "errors": [] }

parameters.name: resource_type
parameters.description: The type of CEDAR resource. The allowed values are: 'field', 'element', 'template', 'instance'

post.description: Create a folder.

get.description: Get a folder.

get.summary: Get the contents of a folder
get.description: Get the contents of a folder.

parameters.name: resource_types
parameters.description: Resource types as comma separated values. The allowed values are: 'folder', 'field', 'element', 'template', 'instance'

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'latest', 'all'

parameters.name: publication_status
parameters.description: Publication status selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'bibo:draft', 'bibo:published', 'all'

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field names as comma separated values. Prepending a field with '-' means descending order on that field. The allowed values are: 'name', 'lastUpdatedOnTS', 'createdOnTS'

get.summary: Get the content extracts of a folder
get.description: Get the content extracts of a folder. Only the enumerated fields will be returned. Multilevel field paths are not supported. It is intended to return smaller payload if a lot of artifacts are expected to be returned.

parameters.name: resource_types
parameters.description: Resource types as comma separated values. The allowed values are: 'folder', 'field', 'element', 'template', 'instance'

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'latest', 'all'

parameters.name: publication_status
parameters.description: Publication status selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'bibo:draft', 'bibo:published', 'all'

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field names as comma separated values. Prepending a field with '-' means descending order on that field. The allowed values are: 'name', 'lastUpdatedOnTS', 'createdOnTS'

parameters.name: field_names
parameters.description: Field name list, separated by comma.

get.summary: Get the details of a folder
get.description: Get the details of a folder.
Get permissions of a folder.

Search resources using different criteria. All of the parameters are optional, but you need to provide at least one search criteria. The parameters can be combined, but not all of the combinations will work. You can see the type of the executed search in the response body.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search term. It will be looked up in the artifact names

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Artifact id. If passed, only the artifact or folder with the given id will be returned.

parameters.name: resource_types
parameters.description: Resource types as comma separated values. The allowed values are: 'folder', 'field', 'element', 'template', 'instance'

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'latest', 'all'

parameters.name: publication_status
parameters.description: Publication status selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'bibo:draft', 'bibo:published', 'all'

parameters.name: is_based_on
parameters.description: Template identifier. All the instances with this template id will be returned

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field names as comma separated values. Prepending a field with '-' means descending order on that field. The allowed values are: 'name', 'lastUpdatedOnTS', 'createdOnTS'

parameters.name: sharing
parameters.description: Sharing modifier for the search. Only the artifacts and folder matching the criteria will be returned.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Search mode. The only value currently supported is 'special-folders'. If passed, the list of special folders will be returned ('/Shared', etc.)

parameters.name: category_id
parameters.description: Category Id. All the artifacts in the given category will be returned.

get.description: Search resources using different criteria. This call can go further than 10.000 resources. It is not intended for everyday use. All of the parameters are optional, but you need to provide at least one search criteria. The parameters can be combined, but not all of the combinations will work. You can see the type of the executed search in the response body.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search term. It will be looked up in the artifact names

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Artifact id. If passed, only the artifact or folder with the given id will be returned.

parameters.name: resource_types
parameters.description: Resource types as comma separated values. The allowed values are: 'folder', 'field', 'element', 'template', 'instance'

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Version selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'latest', 'all'

parameters.name: publication_status
parameters.description: Publication status selector. It is only handled for template-fields, template-elements and templates. The allowed values are: 'bibo:draft', 'bibo:published', 'all'

parameters.name: is_based_on
parameters.description: Template identifier. All the instances with this template id will be returned

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field names as comma separated values. Prepending a field with '-' means descending order on that field. The allowed values are: 'name', 'lastUpdatedOnTS', 'createdOnTS'

parameters.name: sharing
parameters.description: Sharing modifier for the search. Only the artifacts and folder matching the criteria will be returned.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Search mode. The only value currently supported is 'special-folders'. If passed, the list of special folders will be returned ('/Shared', etc.)

parameters.name: category_id
parameters.description: Category Id. All the artifacts in the given category will be returned.

@id.description: Unique URL identifier representing a specific category.

categoryIds.description: Unique URL identifier list representing the categories.

artifactId.description: Unique URL identifier representing the artifact.

categoryId.description: Unique URL identifier representing the category.

@id.description: Unique URL identifier representing the source artifact.

nameTemplate.description: Template that will be used to name the new artifact. The variables in the template will be interpolated. Currently only one variable is supported, the name of the source artifact as '{{name}}'. If no interpolatioin is needed, the new name should be passed.

targetFolderId.description: Unique URL identifier representing the target folder that the new artifact will be created in.

@id.description: Unique URL identifier representing the source artifact.

folderId.description: Unique URL identifier representing the target folder that the new artifact will be created in.

newVersion.description: Version string. Must have three positive decimal numbers, separated by period.

propagateSharing.description: Copy sharing settings, or leave the new artifact unshared

id.description: Unique URL identifier representing a specific folder.

newVersion.description: Version string. Must have three positive decimal numbers, separated by period.
